3. Call Tauri `import_cad_file` command
4. Show import progress and file info

6. Error handling with user-friendly messages
7. File history (last 5 imported files)
8. Use existing design tokens and UI components

Props:
```typescript

  onMeshImported: (mesh: MeshData) => void;
  onError: (error: string) => void;
}
```

parameters.text:
You are creating a MeshViewer component for PROMIN Antenna Studio (React Three Fiber).

Existing viewport:
{{ $('Read Context').item.json.context.viewport }}

Create `MeshViewer.tsx` — 3D mesh visualization:
1. Display imported mesh (triangles + wire segments)
2. Color coding: triangles = surface (blue), segments = wires (copper)
3. Toggle wireframe/solid/transparent modes
4. Highlight selected elements
5. Show mesh quality overlay (aspect ratio heatmap)
6. Display current density magnitude as color map (after solve)
7. Orbit controls (already in viewport)
8. Grid + axis helpers

Use React Three Fiber (@react-three/fiber, @react-three/drei).

```typescript
interface MeshViewerProps {
  mesh: MeshData | null;
  currents?: ComplexArray;
  mode: 'wireframe' | 'solid' | 'transparent';
  showQuality: boolean;
}
```

<file path="src/viewport/MeshViewer.tsx">
...complete React Three Fiber component...
</file>

options.systemMessage: You are a React Three Fiber developer. Build 3D mesh visualization for engineering data. Use drei helpers. Output only the file block.

parameters.text:
You are creating a SolverPanel component for PROMIN Antenna Studio.

Existing App.tsx:
{{ $('Read Context').item.json.context.app }}

Bridge commands:
{{ $('Read Context').item.json.context.bridge }}

Create `SolverPanel.tsx` — solver configuration and control:
1. Solver type selection: MoM Wire / MoM Surface / FDTD
2. Mesh resolution slider (elements per wavelength: 5-30)
3. Frequency settings: single freq, sweep range, or preset
4. Solver options:
   - Linear solver: LU / GMRES
   - Tolerance (for GMRES)
   - Max iterations
5. Run button with progress indicator
6. Cancel button
7. Results summary panel:
   - Z_in (re + im)
   - S11 (dB)
   - VSWR
   - Gain (dBi)
   - Computation time
   - Convergence info
8. Comparison mode: run MoM and FDTD, show side-by-side

```typescript
interface SolverPanelProps {
  antennaType: string;
  antennaParams: Record<string, number>;
  onSolveComplete: (result: SimulationResult) => void;
  onSweepComplete: (result: SweepResult) => void;
}
```

...complete React component...
</file>

parameters.text:
You are creating SolverProgress and ResultsView components for PROMIN Antenna Studio.

Create two components:

### SolverProgress.tsx
- Real-time progress bar during simulation
- Stage indicator (Meshing → Building Z-matrix → Solving → Post-processing)
- ETA display
- Memory usage indicator
- Cancel button
- Polls `get_simulation_status` Tauri command

### ResultsView.tsx
- Comprehensive results display after simulation
- Tabs: Summary | Impedance | S-Parameters | Pattern | Currents
- Summary: key metrics (Z_in, S11, VSWR, Gain, BW)
- Impedance: Z vs frequency plot
- S-Parameters: S11 magnitude/phase
- Pattern: link to radiation pattern tab
- Currents: current distribution on mesh
- Export button (Touchstone S1P)

<file path="src/components/SolverPanel/SolverProgress.tsx">
...complete component...
</file>
<file path="src/components/SolverPanel/ResultsView.tsx">
...complete component...
</file>

Update App.tsx:
1. Add imports for new components
2. Add state for: importedMesh, solverResult, solverMode, isSimulating
3. Add new tab: "Solver" (next to existing tabs)
4. In Solver tab: show SolverPanel + SolverProgress
5. Add file import button/area in sidebar or toolbar
6. When mesh imported: show in MeshViewer (replace or overlay on AntennaViewport)
7. When solve complete: update all charts (S11, Smith, Radiation) with real data
8. Keep ALL existing functionality working!
9. Add keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+R = Run simulation

IMPORTANT: Keep the existing analytical solver as fallback. Add a toggle "Solver Engine: Analytical / MoM / FDTD"
